quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 6, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Nomenclature Practice: Mixed Compound Types
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Naming and Formulas for Ionic Compounds, Covalent Compounds, Acids, Polyatomic Ions, Transition Metals, Stock System

questions.question: Vanadium (III) sulfide is represented as:
questions.explanation: Vanadium(III) sulfide is $\text{V}_2\text{S}_3$. Vanadium(III) has a +3 charge ($\text{V}^{3+}$), and sulfide is $\text{S}^{2-}$ with a -2 charge. To balance charges, two $\text{V}^{3+}$ (+6) combine with three $\text{S}^{2-}$ (-6).

questions.question: $\text{HIO}_3$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{HIO}_3$ is named iodic acid. It contains the iodate ion ($\text{IO}_3^-$). The pattern for iodine oxyacids follows: $\text{IO}^-$ (hypoiodite) → hypoiodous acid, $\text{IO}_2^-$ (iodite) → iodous acid, $\text{IO}_3^-$ (iodate) → iodic acid, $\text{IO}_4^-$ (periodate) → periodic acid. Hydroiodic acid (B) would be $\text{HI}$ (binary acid).

questions.question: $\text{Co}_2(\text{HPO}_4)_3$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{Co}_2(\text{HPO}_4)_3$ is named cobalt(III) monohydrogen phosphate. It contains the monohydrogen phosphate ion ($\text{HPO}_4^{2-}$). The Roman numeral (III) indicates cobalt has a +3 charge.

questions.question: Hydroxide ion is represented as:
questions.explanation: The hydroxide ion is represented as $\text{OH}^-$. It consists of one oxygen atom and one hydrogen atom with an overall 1- charge.

questions.question: $\text{Zn(ClO}_4)_2$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{Zn(ClO}_4)_2$ is named zinc perchlorate. It contains the perchlorate ion ($\text{ClO}_4^-$). Perchlorate has one more oxygen than chlorate ($\text{ClO}_3^-$).

questions.question: $\text{Li}_2\text{O}$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{Li}_2\text{O}$ is named lithium oxide. It is an ionic compound containing the oxide ion ($\text{O}^{2-}$). Lithium (Group 1) forms only one type of ion (+1), so no Roman numeral is needed.

questions.question: Which is the correct formula for iron(II) phosphate?
questions.explanation: Iron(II) phosphate is $\text{Fe}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$. Iron(II) has a +2 charge ($\text{Fe}^{2+}$), and phosphate is $\text{PO}_4^{3-}$ with a -3 charge. To balance charges, three $\text{Fe}^{2+}$ (+6) combine with two $\text{PO}_4^{3-}$ (-6). $\text{Fe(PO}_4)_2$ (C) would incorrectly imply iron has a +4 charge, and $\text{Fe(PO}_3)_2$ (D) would be iron(II) phosphite.

questions.question: The formula for calcium phosphate is:
questions.explanation: Calcium phosphate is $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$. Calcium ($\text{Ca}^{2+}$) has a +2 charge, and phosphate ($\text{PO}_4^{3-}$) has a -3 charge. To balance charges, three $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ (+6) combine with two $\text{PO}_4^{3-}$ (-6). $\text{Ca}_3\text{P}_2$ (D) is calcium phosphide, and $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_3)_2$ (E) is calcium phosphite.

questions.question: Which of the following is the formula for hydroiodic acid?
questions.explanation: Hydroiodic acid is $\text{HI}$. It is a binary acid (hydrogen combined with a halogen). The prefix 'hydro-' and suffix '-ic' indicate a binary acid. $\text{HIO}_4$ (A) is periodic acid, $\text{HIO}_3$ (B) is iodic acid, and $\text{HIO}$ (C) is hypoiodous acid — all are oxyacids containing oxygen.

questions.question: $\text{N}_2$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{N}_2$ is named nitrogen gas. It is a diatomic molecule of the element nitrogen. Nitrate (A) is $\text{NO}_3^-$, nitride (B) is $\text{N}^{3-}$, and nitrium (C) is not a standard term.

questions.question: $\text{O}_2^{2-}$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{O}_2^{2-}$ is named the peroxide ion. It consists of two oxygen atoms with an overall 2- charge. The dioxide ion (A) is not a standard term, and oxygen ion (B) typically refers to $\text{O}^{2-}$ (oxide).

questions.question: Carbon diselenide is represented as:
questions.explanation: Carbon diselenide is $\text{CSe}_2$. The prefix 'di-' indicates two selenium atoms. It is a covalent compound analogous to carbon disulfide ($\text{CS}_2$).

questions.question: $\text{PbF}_4$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{PbF}_4$ is named lead(IV) fluoride. Fluorine has a -1 charge, so four fluorines give -4. Therefore, lead must have a +4 charge, indicated by (IV).

questions.question: $\text{Mg}_3\text{N}_2$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{Mg}_3\text{N}_2$ is named magnesium nitride. It contains the nitride ion ($\text{N}^{3-}$). Magnesium (Group 2) forms only one type of ion (+2), so no Roman numeral is needed.

questions.question: $\text{Cr(H}_2\text{PO}_4)_3$ is represented as:
questions.explanation: $\text{Cr(H}_2\text{PO}_4)_3$ is named chromium(III) dihydrogen phosphate. It contains the dihydrogen phosphate ion ($\text{H}_2\text{PO}_4^-$). The Roman numeral (III) indicates chromium has a +3 charge.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered a wide range of nomenclature skills! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review polyatomic ions and their charges.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between similar-sounding ion names (-ate, -ite, -ide).
scoreMessages.0: Please review naming rules for different types of compounds including ionic, covalent, and acids.
